label: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs

node.label: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs
node.definition: Bayesian networks, Markov random fields, and factor graphs factorize a joint distribution into local relationships that support modular probabilistic inference.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs is modeled as depending on Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference and feeding downstream nodes such as Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Graph theory, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.

In this atlas graph, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Bayesian & approximate inference because Bayesian & approximate inference is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Bayesian networks, MRFs & factor graphs has an `optimizes_for` dependency on MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing because MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
